scope.target: Rename legacy shell script ownership to .NET-first names while preserving compatibility wrappers
scope.dependency_radius: Script entry points plus any docs/prompts that reference the legacy names
constraints: Keep old names working during this task
Do not expand scope to unrelated script cleanup
non_goals: No mass rename across all scripts
No removal of compatibility wrappers in this stage

inputs.architecture_target: Make .NET-first script names canonical and reduce historical naming confusion
review_findings: Current .NET-friendly script names delegate into legacy spring/jpa filenames
README still documents the legacy files as compatibility wrappers
user_constraints: Store the plan so work can resume on another computer
Do not lose the analyzed working mode or task structure

steps: Inventory references to the three legacy script names
Flip ownership so .NET-first names become real implementations
Convert legacy names into thin wrappers
Update README and any remaining references
Verify wrapper direction and search results
validation: Canonical files no longer delegate into legacy names
deferred_items: Potential later removal of legacy wrappers after migration period
Potential broader cleanup of other historical script names

results.summary: Flipped script ownership so the .NET-first names are the real implementations, converted the legacy names into thin compatibility wrappers, and updated script documentation to promote the canonical entry points.
